LAW AND MOTION 302,PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE CASE CGC-15-548732 AND CGC-17-562136 AND FOR IMPOSITION OF CREDITOR LIEN UPON DEFENDANT ZI CHENG, LLC. PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE CASE NOS. CGC-15-548732 AND CGC-17-562136 IS DENIED. CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1048 CONTEMPLATES CONSOLIDATION OF PENDING ACTIONS EITHER FOR PRETRIAL PURPOSES OR FOR TRIAL TO ENHANCE TRIAL COURT EFFICIENCY AND TO AVOID THE DANGER OF INCONSISTENT ADJUDICATIONS. HERE, THE TWO ACTIONS INVOLVE DISTINCT CLAIMS AGAINST DIFFERENT DEFENDANTS-THE FIRST IS NO LONGER PENDING IN THAT FINAL JUDGMENT HAS ALREADY BEEN ENTERED, AND THE SECOND HAS NOT YET PROCEEDED TO TRIAL. CONSOLIDATION IS NOT AUTHORIZED OR WARRANTED UNDER SUCH CIRCUMSTANCES. PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE'S MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF A CREDITOR LIEN UPON DEFENDANT ZI CHENG, LLC IS DENIED. NO MONEY JUDGMENT HAS BEEN ENTERED AGAINST ZI CHENG, LLC THAT COULD SERVE AS THE BASIS FOR SUCH A LIEN. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANTS IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
ORDER OF EXAMINATION ISSUED
Off Calendar Apr-04-2019 Off Calendar

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-17-562136 WITH CASE CGC-15-548732
Off Calendar Mar-11-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE CASE CGC-15-548732 and CGC-17-562136 and for imposition of creditor lien upon defendant Zi Cheng, LLC.

Plaintiff Julie Lee's motion to consolidate Case Nos. CGC-15-548732 and CGC-17-562136 is denied. Code of Civil Procedure section 1048 contemplates consolidation of pending actions either for pretrial purposes or for trial to enhance trial court efficiency and to avoid the danger of inconsistent adjudications. Here, the two actions involve distinct claims against different defendants-the first is no longer pending in that final judgment has already been entered, and the second has not yet proceeded to trial. Consolidation is not authorized or warranted under such circumstances.

Plaintiff Julie Lee's motion for imposition of a creditor lien upon Defendant Zi Cheng, LLC is denied. No money judgment has been entered against Zi Cheng, LLC that could serve as the basis for such a lien.

Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
